title: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Public Records Request — Denver Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Records Administrator
City and County of Denver
201 W. Colfax Avenue
Denver, CO 80202

Dear Records Administrator,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to the City and County of Denver's use of generative AI tools by city employees, including Microsoft Copilot Chat and Ask Dee (AiseraGPT).

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, C.R.S. § 24-72-203(3)(b), we are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Ask Dee (AiseraGPT), Microsoft Copilot Chat, Sunny AI, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by City and County of Denver employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing City and County of Denver employee use of Ask Dee, Microsoft Copilot Chat, Sunny AI, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The City and County of Denver has deployed Ask Dee, powered by AiseraGPT, which uses domain-specific large language models to automatically resolve employee IT support requests such as software provisioning, account troubleshooting, and hardware issues, auto-resolving 81% of inquiries (see Aisera, "City & County of Denver Customer Story," https://aisera.com/customers/city-and-county-of-denver/). Denver's Chief AI and Information Officer Suma Nallapati has overseen the development of the Sunny AI platform and the city's partnership with Microsoft on Copilot Chat for employee use (see Harvard Data-Smart City Solutions, "Personalizing Government at Scale: Denver's AI Strategy," https://datasmart.hks.harvard.edu/personalizing-denver-ai; see also GovTech, "How a New Title for Denver's CIO Helps Power AI Work," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/how-a-new-title-for-denvers-cio-helps-power-ai-work). Denver has also issued an RFP seeking skilled AI vendors to drive innovation and improve the resident experience (see City and County of Denver, "AI RFP Press Release," https://denvergov.org/Government/Agencies-Departments-Offices/Agencies-Departments-Offices-Directory/Technology-Services/News/AI-RFP-Press-Release).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Ask Dee (AiseraGPT) maintains server-side conversation logs and resolution data accessible through the Aisera administrative dashboard. For Microsoft Copilot Chat, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Colorado has enacted one of the nation's most comprehensive AI records mandates. The Colorado AI Act (SB 24-205, signed 2024, effective February 1, 2026) requires deployers of high-risk AI systems — including government agencies — to implement risk management policies, conduct impact assessments, and maintain documentation and records of AI system use and outcomes. C.R.S. § 6-1-1701 et seq. The Colorado Open Records Act defines "public records" to include all writings made or maintained by any state agency regardless of format. C.R.S. § 24-72-202. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Officer
North Carolina Department of Information Technology
4101 Mail Service Center
Raleigh, NC 27699-4101

Dear Public Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to North Carolina's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including Microsoft 365 Copilot and ChatGPT Enterprise.

We are requesting the following records under N.C.G.S. § 132-6(a):

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, and other generative AI tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

North Carolina has been a national leader in AI governance. Governor Stein signed Executive Order No. 24 establishing statewide principles, governance, and accountability for AI use, and NCDIT appointed the state's first Deputy Secretary for AI and Policy (see Governor Josh Stein, "Executive Order No. 24: Advancing Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence," Sept. 2, 2025, https://governor.nc.gov/executive-order-no-24-advancing-trustworthy-artificial-intelligence-benefits-all-north-carolinians; see also NCDIT, "2025: A Landmark Year for NCDIT," Jan. 12, 2026, https://it.nc.gov/news/press-releases/2026/01/12/2025-landmark-year-ncdit-ai-innovation-connectivity-and-security-advancements). The N.C. Department of State Treasurer conducted a 12-week ChatGPT Enterprise pilot with 36 employees in partnership with OpenAI, with 85% reporting a positive experience (see GovTech, "North Carolina Hacks the Treasury Bureaucracy With ChatGPT," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/north-carolina-hacks-the-treasury-bureaucracy-with-chatgpt; see also NC Treasurer, "Lessons from NC Treasurer's Office Generative AI Pilot with ChatGPT," https://www.nctreasurer.gov/nccunc-treasurer-ai-survey-report/open). NCDIT has introduced an AI User Code of Conduct and established an AI Accelerator as a centralized hub for AI governance.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. North Carolina's Public Records Act defines "public record" to include all documentary material regardless of physical form or characteristics, including "electronic data-processing records," made or received in connection with official business. N.C.G.S. § 132-1. Governor Stein's Executive Order No. 31 (2025) directed agencies to inventory AI uses and develop responsible AI policies. The NC Department of Information Technology has issued AI use policies requiring documentation of AI tools deployed, human oversight of AI-assisted decisions, and retention of AI-generated materials as public records. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

FOIA Officer
Virginia Information Technologies Agency (VITA)
7325 Beaufont Springs Drive
Richmond, VA 23225

Dear FOIA Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Virginia's use of generative AI tools by state employees.

We are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for any generative AI chatbot tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Governor Youngkin signed Executive Order 30, and VITA subsequently published the "Utilization of Artificial Intelligence by the Commonwealth of Virginia" policy standard governing AI use across state agencies (see https://www.vita.virginia.gov/media/vitavirginiagov/it-governance/ea/pdf/Utilization-of-Artificial-Intelligence-by-COV-Policy-Standard.pdf). VITA's AI registry has approved 112 AI use cases submitted by state agencies (see Virginia Mercury, "Virginia's artificial intelligence registry faces transparency challenges, study shows," Nov. 12, 2025, https://virginiamercury.com/2025/11/12/virginias-artificial-intelligence-registry-faces-transparency-challenges-study-shows/). Copilot Chat and the AI translation tool Smartling have been made available to all executive branch agencies, with VITA offering virtual training sessions on Copilot Chat for Commonwealth employees (see https://www.vita.virginia.gov/news--events/latest-events/name-1057122-en.html).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address or phone number below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, Va. Code 2.2-3700 et seq., public records include "all writings and recordings that consist of letters, words or numbers, or their equivalent, set down by handwriting, typewriting, printing, photostatting, photography, magnetic impulse, optical or magneto-optical form, mechanical or electronic recording or other form of data compilation, however stored, and regardless of physical form or characteristics, prepared or owned by, or in the possession of a public body or its officers, employees or agents in the transaction of public business." Va. Code 2.2-3701. AI chatbot interaction logs used by state employees in their official duties qualify as public records under this definition. Executive Order 30 and VITA's AI policy further confirm that these AI tools are being deployed as part of official state operations. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Officer
City of Tucson
255 W. Alameda Street
Tucson, AZ 85701

Dear Public Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to the City of Tucson's use of generative AI tools by city employees, including Axon Draft One and generative AI tools used for permit processing.

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, A.R.S. § 39-121.01(D)(1), we are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Axon Draft One, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by City of Tucson employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to city employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Tucson's public safety teams are piloting Axon's Draft One, which leverages generative AI and body-worn camera audio to produce draft police report narratives, with deputies reporting approximately one hour saved each day (see CNN, "How AI is being used by police departments to help draft reports," Aug. 12, 2025, https://www.cnn.com/2025/08/12/tech/ai-police-reports-axon; see also Axon, "Draft One," https://www.axon.com/products/draft-one). Administrative staff also use generative and predictive AI tools to speed up permits, translations, and data analysis. The City's Advanced Technology Committee and Technology & Data Policies require supplemental reviews, human oversight, bias checks, and director signoff before deploying AI systems (see Nucamp, "The Complete Guide to Using AI in the Government Industry in Tucson in 2025," https://www.nucamp.co/blog/coding-bootcamp-tucson-az-government-the-complete-guide-to-using-ai-in-the-government-industry-in-tucson-in-2025).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Axon Draft One generates draft police reports from body-worn camera audio; the initial AI-generated drafts and any officer edits may be retained in the Axon Evidence system. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Arizona's public records law requires that public bodies promptly furnish copies of public records to any person requesting them. A.R.S. § 39-121.01(D)(1). Arizona courts have broadly interpreted "public records" to include electronic communications and digital records created in the transaction of public business. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Officer
City of San Jose
200 E. Santa Clara Street
San Jose, CA 95113

Dear Public Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to San Jose's use of generative AI tools by city employees.

We are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from ChatGPT Teams, Microsoft Copilot, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by city employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing city employee use of ChatGPT Teams, Microsoft Copilot, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for ChatGPT Teams and any other generative AI chatbot tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

San Jose has purchased 89 ChatGPT Teams licenses at $400 per account, spending more than $35,000, for city employees to use generative AI in their work (see Washington Post, "ChatGPT helps write this mayor's speeches. Now he wants a thousand city workers using AI," July 17, 2025, https://www.washingtonpost.com/business/2025/07/17/ai-at-work-san-jose-mayor-matt-mahan-chatgpt/2663b12a-6313-11f0-bf70-56d8888ebb94_story.html; see also Fortune, "Liberal mayor proudly outsources part of his job to ChatGPT," July 17, 2025, https://fortune.com/2025/07/17/san-jose-mayor-matt-mahan-uses-chatgpt-ai-to-do-job/). Mayor Matt Mahan has publicly described using ChatGPT for his own work, and by 2026 he aims to have about 15% of the city's nearly 7,000 workers trained to use AI. San Jose has published generative AI guidelines for city employees (see https://www.sanjoseca.gov/your-government/departments-offices/information-technology/itd-generative-ai-guideline) and is a founder of the GovAI Coalition, a network of government agencies promoting responsible AI use (see https://www.sanjoseca.gov/your-government/departments-offices/information-technology/ai-reviews-algorithm-register/govai-coalition).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address or phone number below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT Teams conversation histories can be exported by workspace administrators through the ChatGPT admin console, or by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the California Public Records Act, Cal. Gov. Code 6253(c), public records include "any writing containing information relating to the conduct of the public's business prepared, owned, used, or retained by any state or local agency regardless of physical form or characteristics." Cal. Gov. Code 6252(e). AI chatbot interaction logs created or used by city employees in the course of their official duties qualify as public records under this definition. The city's published generative AI guidelines and its procurement of 89 ChatGPT Teams licenses confirm these tools are part of the conduct of public business. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Pennsylvania's Right-to-Know Law defines "record" as information regardless of physical form that documents a transaction or activity of an agency. 65 P.S. § 67.102. The Office of Open Records has confirmed that electronic communications on all platforms constitute public records. Governor Shapiro's Executive Order 2024-02 established a Generative AI Governance Board, and the Office of Information Technology issued GenAI guidelines directing state employees to document AI tool usage and treat AI-generated outputs as agency records. Trenton, NJ 08625

We are writing to request records related to New Jersey's use of generative AI tools by state employees, including the NJ AI Assistant.

We are requesting the following records under N.J.S.A. 47:1A-5(i):

1. All interaction logs from the NJ AI Assistant, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of the NJ AI Assistant or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for the NJ AI Assistant showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The NJ AI Assistant, built in-house by the New Jersey Office of Innovation, has been used by more than 15,000 state employees — over one in five — with over 300,000 sessions and 1 million prompts as of February 2026, and user satisfaction exceeding 80% (see NJ Office of Innovation, "NJ AI Assistant," https://innovation.nj.gov/projects/ai-assistant/; see also Route Fifty, "How New Jersey's AI assistant saves the state time and money," Aug. 2025, https://www.route-fifty.com/artificial-intelligence/2025/08/how-new-jerseys-ai-assistant-saves-state-time-and-money/407538/). We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, "WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents" (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. New Jersey's Open Public Records Act defines "government record" as any record made, maintained, or kept on file in the course of official business, including electronic data. N.J.S.A. 47:1A-1 et seq. The Government Records Council has repeatedly held that electronic communications are government records. Governor Murphy's Executive Order No. 346 (2024) established an AI Task Force, and the Office of Information Technology issued interim GenAI guidance requiring state employees to document when AI tools are used to generate official communications and to treat AI-generated work product as government records under OPRA. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

We are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, ChatGPT, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by state employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing state employee use of Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, ChatGPT, or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for any generative AI chatbot tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Governor Moore signed Executive Order 01.01.2024.02, "Catalyzing the Responsible and Productive Use of Artificial Intelligence in Maryland State Government" (see https://governor.maryland.gov/Lists/ExecutiveOrders/Attachments/31/EO%2001.01.2024.02). The Maryland General Assembly subsequently enacted the AI Governance Act of 2024 (SB 818), providing statutory backing for AI oversight and reporting across state agencies (see https://mgaleg.maryland.gov/mgawebsite/Legislation/Details/SB0818?ys=2024rs). DoIT established the Office of AI Enablement and published a 2025 AI Enablement Strategy (see https://doit.maryland.gov/policies/ai/Pages/maryland-AI-enablement-strategy-and-roadmap.aspx). Fifty-nine state agencies are now using Google Workspace with Gemini, providing nearly 43,000 state employees access to Gemini's AI tools, with 12,500 active users already testing its capabilities (see GovTech, "Maryland Expands AI Integration Across State Government," https://www.govtech.com/artificial-intelligence/maryland-expands-ai-integration-across-state-government). Maryland has also piloted Microsoft Copilot for state employee use.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address or phone number below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, workspace administrators may access usage logs through the Google Admin console. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Maryland Public Information Act, Md. State Gov't 10-611 et seq., public records include "the original or any copy of any documentary material that is made by a unit or instrumentality of the State government or of a political subdivision, is received by a unit or instrumentality of the State government or of a political subdivision in connection with the transaction of public business, and is in any form." Md. State Gov't 10-611(g)(1). AI chatbot interaction logs created or used by state employees in the course of their official duties qualify as public records under this definition. Executive Order 01.01.2024.02 and the AI Governance Act of 2024 (SB 818) further establish that AI tools are part of official state government operations subject to oversight and reporting. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

We are writing to request records related to Cook County's use of generative AI and machine learning tools by county employees, including the Assessor's Office automated valuation models.

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, 5 ILCS 140/3, we are requesting the following records:

1. 3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to county employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Cook County Assessor's Office has been a national leader in transparent use of AI and machine learning for government operations. Under Assessor Fritz Kaegi, the office publicly released its residential assessment and modeling code as well as underlying datasets on GitLab and the Cook County Open Data Portal, making Cook County the first assessor's office in the United States known to open-source its automated valuation models (see Cook County Assessor's Office, "Cook County Assessor's Office Publicly Releases Residential Assessment Code and Models," https://www.cookcountyassessoril.gov/news/cook-county-assessors-office-publicly-releases-residential-assessment-code-and-models; see also Route Fifty, "County assessor's code repository powers transparent government," https://gcn.com/cloud-infrastructure/2022/08/county-assessors-code-repository-powers-transparent-government/376220/). The office's model-res-avm uses machine learning trained on property characteristics and sales data to generate assessed values for all residential properties (see GitHub, Cook County Assessor's Office, https://github.com/ccao-data). The Assessor's Office also released its 2025 Open Data Refresh with newly available data assets (see Cook County Open Data, "Assessor — 2025 Open Data Refresh," https://datacatalog.cookcountyil.gov/stories/s/Assessor-2025-Open-Data-Refresh/gzdr-q7c4/).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, administrators can access interaction logs through the Google Workspace Admin Console under Reporting > Audit and Investigation. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Illinois's Freedom of Information Act explicitly enumerates "electronic data processing records, electronic communications, and recorded information of any kind" as public records. 5 ILCS 140/2(c). The Public Access Counselor within the AG's office has issued binding opinions confirming that electronic messages are public records. Governor Pritzker's Executive Order 2024-07 directed the creation of an AI governance framework including documentation requirements. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics for ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, or other generative AI tools showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. For Google Gemini, interaction data may be available through Google Vault or Google Takeout. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Connecticut's Freedom of Information Act defines "public records" as any recorded data or information relating to the conduct of the public's business, including electronic data. Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-200(5). Public Act 24-130 (2024) requires state agencies to complete an inventory of all AI systems used in consequential decision-making, including documentation of purpose, data inputs, and outcomes. Governor Lamont's Executive Order No. 1 (2023) directed the development of an AI policy framework emphasizing transparency and accountability. Alexander Spangher

Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Atlanta City Council established an Artificial Intelligence Commission in December 2024 to develop potential regulatory legislation and explore opportunities for using AI in city government, including improved public safety, infrastructure monitoring, emergency response, and customer service enhancements such as multilingual 311 chatbots (see Smart Cities Dive, "Atlanta creates AI commission to explore tech's potential in city government," https://www.smartcitiesdive.com/news/atlanta-creates-ai-commission/734537/; see also Atlanta City Council, "Atlanta Artificial Intelligence Commission Set to Hold First Meeting," https://citycouncil.atlantaga.gov/Home/Components/News/News/4162/17). The city currently uses AI for social media posts and an AI chatbot for its 311 service, and police departments across Georgia and the nation have adopted AI tools such as Axon's Draft One to generate draft police reports from body-worn camera audio (see CNN, "How AI is being used by police departments to help draft reports," Aug. 12, 2025, https://www.cnn.com/2025/08/12/tech/ai-police-reports-axon; see also EFF, "AI Police Reports: Year In Review," Dec. 2025, https://www.eff.org/deeplinks/2025/12/ai-police-reports-year-review).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Axon Draft One generates draft police reports from body-worn camera audio; the initial AI-generated drafts and any officer edits may be retained in the Axon Evidence system. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Georgia's Open Records Act defines "public record" to include all documents and "computer-based or generated information" prepared, maintained, or received by an agency in the course of operations. O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70(a). Governor Kemp's Executive Order 07.25.23.02 (2023) created a Commission on AI in state government, and the Georgia Technology Authority has issued AI use guidance recommending documentation of AI deployments and treatment of AI-generated work product as public records. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.

title: Public Records Request — AlamedaCounty Generative AI Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request — AlamedaCounty Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 9, 2026

Public Records Officer
Alameda County
1221 Oak Street, Room 536
Oakland, CA 94612

Dear Public Records Officer,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Alameda County's use of generative AI tools by county employees, including any GPT-4-based AI assistants.

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, Cal. Gov. Code § 6253(c), we are requesting the following records:

1. All interaction logs from any GPT-4 or GPT-based AI assistant, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by Alameda County employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing Alameda County employee use of generative AI chatbot tools, including the County's Generative Artificial Intelligence Policy adopted September 1, 2024.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

4. Administrative usage statistics showing number of users, sessions, and queries processed for any generative AI tools deployed to county employees.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Alameda County adopted a formal Generative Artificial Intelligence Policy on September 1, 2024, to govern the acquisition and use of GenAI software across all county agencies and departments. The policy appoints the Chief Information Officer and the Information Technology Department as responsible for defining and developing guidelines to maximize benefits and mitigate risks of GenAI use (see Alameda County ITD, "Generative AI Policy," https://itd.alamedacountyca.gov/generative-ai-policy/). The County has also deployed a GPT-4-based Board AI Assistant for use by county officials. The National Association of Counties highlighted Alameda County's AI innovations as part of its survey of county-level GenAI use cases (see NACo, "Artificial Intelligence and GenAI In Motion: County Innovations and Use Cases," https://www.naco.org/resource/artificial-intelligence-and-genai-motion-county-innovations-and-use-cases).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that GPT-4-based assistants deployed through Azure OpenAI may have interaction logs accessible via the Azure Monitor Log Analytics workspace if diagnostic logging with content logging (RequestResponseLogs) is enabled. For ChatGPT, conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. The California Public Records Act defines "public records" to include any writing containing information relating to the conduct of the public's business, regardless of physical form or characteristics, encompassing electronic communications and digital files. Cal. Gov. Code § 7920.530. Governor Newsom's Executive Order N-12-23 (September 2023) directed state agencies to develop a generative AI framework, and the CalGovOps GenAI Policy Framework (2024) requires agencies to maintain inventories of AI/GenAI tools. Alameda County has adopted a formal GenAI policy (September 2024), reflecting the expectation that AI interactions are documented and subject to public records obligations. Consistent with this trend nationally, NARA has made clear that chats and other electronic messages used for agency business are likely government records. NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02.
